📖 Hamilton Ventilator Modes Reference
tap to expand
🧟 Hamilton Ventilator Import PHOTO → DATA
📷 Capture Vent Screen
No data

Take a photo of the Hamilton ventilator display. AI will extract mode, Vt, RR, PEEP, FiO₂, PIP, Pplat, I:E ratio, and compliance. Supports Hamilton C1, C3, C6, T1, MR1, G5.

⚙ Actual Ventilator Settings MANUAL INPUT

Enter current ventilator settings. Save with timestamp as Initiated (new settings), Change (adjustment), or add a comment. Entries save to Dashboard & Change Log.

📷 Capture ABG / VBG Report
No data

Photo the blood gas printout or ABG machine screen. AI extracts pH, PaCO₂, PaO₂, HCO₃, BE, Lactate, SaO₂, and electrolytes. Specify ABG or VBG.

🧠 AI Clinical Analysis

Combines vent settings, vital signs, and blood gas data to provide a clinical assessment, identify potential problems, and suggest new ventilator settings. Requires at least vent settings + one other data source.

⚠ Clinical Decision Support Only — AI-generated recommendations are not a substitute for clinical judgment. All ventilator changes must be verified by a qualified provider. This tool is intended as an educational aid and clinical decision support reference.

⚙ API Key Settings
Connect to Anthropic AI — works in any browser once configured
Get your key at console.anthropic.com → API Keys → Create Key
AI Features
✦ Generate Transport Report
✦ IV pump + patient ID photo scan
✦ Drug Library label scan & MOA lookup
✦ Hamilton Vent screen photo import
✦ Vital signs monitor photo import
✦ ABG/VBG photo import & interpretation
✦ AI Vent Clinical Analysis & Recommendations
⚠ Security
Key stored in browser localStorage on this device only. Do not use on shared computers.
Option A — Easiest: Local web server (Python)
Python is installed on most Macs and Windows 10+ machines. Open Terminal / Command Prompt in the folder containing this HTML file and run: 
python3 -m http.server 8080
Then open http://localhost:8080/icu_medication_reference_62.html in your browser.
Your API key (saved in the API Key tab) will work automatically.
Option B — Node.js Proxy (if Python is unavailable)
Save the code below as proxy.js in the same folder, then run it. Requires Node.js installed. 
// proxy.js — save next to your HTML file, then: node proxy.js
const http = require('http');
const https = require('https');
const fs = require('fs');
const KEY = process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY || '';
if (!KEY) { console.error('Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var'); process.exit(1); }
http.createServer((req, res) => {
  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*');
  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Headers', 'Content-Type');
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') { res.writeHead(204); return res.end(); }
  if (req.method === 'POST' && req.url === '/v1/messages') {
    let body = '';
    req.on('data', d => body += d);
    req.on('end', () => {
      const opt = { hostname:'api.anthropic.com', path:'/v1/messages', method:'POST',
        headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json','x-api-key':KEY,'anthropic-version':'2023-06-01'} };
      const pr = https.request(opt, r => {
        res.writeHead(r.statusCode, {'Content-Type':'application/json'});
        r.pipe(res);
      });
      pr.on('error', e => { res.writeHead(500); res.end(JSON.stringify({error:{message:e.message}})); });
      pr.write(body); pr.end();
    });
  } else { res.writeHead(404); res.end(); }
}).listen(3001, () => console.log('Proxy running at http://localhost:3001'));
Run: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... node proxy.js
Then enter http://localhost:3001 in the Proxy URL field below and save.
